info.title: Flashduty Open API
info.description: Public HTTP API for the Flashduty incident management platform — incidents, notification templates, channels, schedules, monitors, RUM, and platform administration. Every operation is authenticated with an `app_key` query parameter issued from the Flashduty console under Account → APP Keys. Responses follow a uniform envelope: `{ request_id, data }` on success, `{ request_id, error }` on failure.

tags.name: Platform/Audit logs
tags.description: Search and retrieve account operation audit logs.

post.description: Return a paginated list of teams in the current account.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | None — any valid `app_key` can call this operation |

## Usage

- Filter by `person_id` to return teams that a specific person belongs to.
- Defaults: p=1, limit=20.

post.description: Disable a custom role to prevent it from granting permissions.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Roles Manage** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- Members who held this role lose its permissions immediately.
- Only custom roles can be disabled.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Permanently delete a custom role and revoke it from all members.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Roles Manage** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- Built-in roles cannot be deleted.
- All members who held this role lose its permissions immediately.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return all available permissions, optionally filtered to those granted to specific roles.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | None — any valid `app_key` can call this operation |

## Usage

- Pass `role_ids` to filter permissions to those granted to those roles.
- Pass `with_all: true` to include all permissions regardless of role filter, with `is_granted` set to indicate which are granted to the specified roles.

post.description: Permanently delete a team by ID, name, or external reference ID.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Teams Manage** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- At least one of `team_id`, `team_name`, or `ref_id` must be provided.
- Fails with `400 ReferenceExist` if the team is still referenced by schedules, escalation rules, or other resources.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return a cursor-paginated list of audit log entries within a time range.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Audit Read** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- Time range is required. Maximum span is 90 days. Both `start_time` and `end_time` are Unix epoch **seconds**.
- Use `search_after_ctx` from the previous response to fetch the next page. The token is opaque — do not construct it manually.
- The retention window depends on the account's license. Queries beyond the retention boundary silently return an empty result rather than an error.
- Default page size is 20 rows; maximum is 99.

post.description: Assign a role to one or more members, giving them its permissions.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Roles Manage** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- Up to 100 member IDs per request.
- Members who already have the role are silently skipped.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Replace all role assignments for a member at once.

post.description: Create a new custom role or update an existing one. Pass `role_id` to update.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Roles Manage** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- Omit `role_id` (or set to 0) to create; pass an existing ID to update.
- `role_name` must be 1–39 characters and unique within the account.
- `permission_ids` sets the full permission set for the role, replacing any previous assignment.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return all permission factors (API, button, menu, URL, visit) optionally filtered by type.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | None — any valid `app_key` can call this operation |

## Usage

- Permission factors are the fine-grained controls that make up each permission.
- `factor_types` accepts: `api`, `button`, `visit`, `menu`, `url`.

post.description: Return all operation names that are recorded in the audit log, for use as `operations` filter values.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Audit Read** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- Use the `name` values from this response as `operations` filter values in `POST /audit/search`.
- `name_cn` is the human-readable Chinese label shown in the console; `name` is the stable wire value to filter on.

post.description: Create a new team or update an existing one. Pass `team_id` to update.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Teams Manage** (`organization`) |

## Usage

- Omit `team_id` (or set to 0) to create a new team; pass an existing ID to update.
- `team_name` must be 1–39 characters and unique within the account.
- Pass `person_ids` to set team membership; this replaces the entire member list.
- Pass `emails` or `phones` to invite members who don't yet have accounts.
- `ref_id` is an external identifier for integration with third-party HR systems.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return a paginated list of organization members.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | None — any valid `app_key` can call this operation |

AppKeyAuth.name: app_key
AppKeyAuth.description: App key issued from the Flashduty console under Account → APP Keys. Required on every public API call. Keep it secret — it grants the same access as the owning account.

BadRequest.description: Invalid request — usually a missing or malformed parameter.

error.message: The specified parameter is not valid.

Forbidden.description: The app_key is valid but lacks permission for this operation.

NotFound.description: The referenced resource does not exist or has been deleted. Note: Flashduty historically returns HTTP 400 with code `ResourceNotFound` for missing domain entities; a true 404 is reserved for unknown routes.

error.message: The resource you request is not found

TooManyRequests.description: Rate limit hit. Either the global API limit, a per-account limit, or a per-integration limit.

ServerError.description: Unexpected server-side error. Include the request_id when reporting.

error.message: We encountered an internal error, and it has been reported. Please try again later.

created_at.description: Timestamp of the operation in Unix epoch milliseconds.

member_id.description: ID of the member who performed the action.

ip.description: Client IP address of the caller.

operation_name.description: Human-readable operation label in the account's locale.

body.description: JSON-encoded request body (may be truncated at 10 KB).

params.description: URL path parameters as an array of key-value pairs, or an empty array when none.

is_dangerous.description: True if this is flagged as a high-risk operation.

is_write.description: True for mutating operations; false for read-only ones.

name.description: Stable machine-readable operation name for use as a filter.

name_cn.description: Human-readable Chinese label shown in the console.

AuditSearchRequest.description: Filter criteria for audit log search. Time range is required.

start_time.description: Start of the search window, Unix epoch seconds.

end_time.description: End of the search window, Unix epoch seconds. Must be after `start_time`. Maximum span 90 days.

request_id.description: Filter to a single request by its unique request ID.

search_after_ctx.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by the previous response. Leave empty for the first page.

operations.description: Filter to specific operation names. Use `POST /audit/operation/list` to get the valid set.

person_id.description: Filter by the member who performed the action.

is_dangerous.description: When true, return only high-risk (dangerous) operations.

is_write.description: When true, return only write operations; when false, return only read operations.

total.description: Total matching entries in the search window.

search_after_ctx.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Empty string when there are no more results.

docs.description: Audit log entries for this page.

DutyError.description: Error payload inside the response envelope. Present only on non-2xx responses.

message.description: Human-readable error message, localized by the caller's Accept-Language. May contain field names, IDs, or other context from the failing request.
message.example: The specified parameter template_id is not valid.

EmptyObject.description: An empty object. Returned as the `data` payload by operations whose success signal is simply the absence of an error.

EmptyResponse.description: Empty response body. The server returns `data: null` on success.

ErrorCode.description:
Flashduty error code enum. Every failed API response sets `error.code` to one of these stable wire strings. HTTP status is informational — the authoritative signal is the enum value.

| Code | HTTP | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `OK` | 200 | Reserved — not returned on real errors. |
| `InvalidParameter` | 400 | A required parameter is missing or failed validation. |
| `BadRequest` | 400 | Generic 400 used when no more specific code fits. |
| `InvalidContentType` | 400 | The `Content-Type` header is not `application/json`. |
| `ResourceNotFound` | 400 | The referenced resource does not exist. Note: returned as HTTP 400, not 404 (historical choice). |
| `NoLicense` | 400 | The feature is license-gated and no active license was found. |
| `ReferenceExist` | 400 | Deletion blocked — other entities still reference this resource. |
| `Unauthorized` | 401 | `app_key` is missing, invalid, or expired. |
| `BalanceNotEnough` | 402 | Billing-gated operation with insufficient account balance. |
| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Authenticated but lacking the permission required for this operation. |
| `RouteNotFound` | 404 | The request URL path is not a known route. |
| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | The HTTP method is not allowed on this otherwise-known path. |
| `UndonedOrderExist` | 409 | An outstanding billing order blocks this new one. Wait and retry. |
| `RequestLocked` | 423 | Operation temporarily locked due to repeated failures. |
| `EntityTooLarge` | 413 | Request body exceeds the configured max size. |
| `RequestTooFrequently` | 429 | Rate limit hit — API-global, per-account, or per-integration. |
| `RequestVerifyRequired` | 428 | Second-factor verification required but not supplied. |
| `DangerousOperation` | 428 | High-risk operation requires MFA verification. |
| `InternalError` | 500 | Unhandled server-side error. Include `request_id` in the bug report. |
| `ServiceUnavailable` | 503 | A backend dependency is unavailable. Try again later. |

x-enumDescriptions.OK: Reserved — not returned on real errors.
x-enumDescriptions.InvalidParameter: A required parameter is missing or failed validation.
x-enumDescriptions.BadRequest: Generic 400 used when no more specific code fits.
x-enumDescriptions.ResourceNotFound: The referenced resource does not exist. Note: returned as HTTP 400, not 404 (historical choice).
x-enumDescriptions.NoLicense: The feature is license-gated and no active license was found.
x-enumDescriptions.ReferenceExist: Deletion blocked — other entities still reference this resource.
x-enumDescriptions.BalanceNotEnough: Billing-gated operation with insufficient account balance.
x-enumDescriptions.AccessDenied: Authenticated but lacking the permission required for this operation.
x-enumDescriptions.RouteNotFound: The request URL path is not a known route.
x-enumDescriptions.MethodNotAllowed: The HTTP method is not allowed on this otherwise-known path.
x-enumDescriptions.UndonedOrderExist: An outstanding billing order blocks this new one. Wait and retry.
x-enumDescriptions.RequestLocked: Operation temporarily locked due to repeated failures.
x-enumDescriptions.EntityTooLarge: Request body exceeds the configured max size.
x-enumDescriptions.RequestTooFrequently: Rate limit hit — API-global, per-account, or per-integration.
x-enumDescriptions.RequestVerifyRequired: Second-factor verification required but not supplied.
x-enumDescriptions.InternalError: Unhandled server-side error. Include `request_id` in the bug report.
x-enumDescriptions.ServiceUnavailable: A backend dependency is unavailable. Try again later.

ErrorResponse.description: Response envelope for errors. `error` is required; `data` is absent.

MemberDeleteRequest.description: Delete member request (provide one of the lookup fields)

account_locale.description: Account-level locale preference (e.g. zh-CN or en-US)

status.description: Member status. `enabled` — active member; `pending` — invited but not yet accepted; `deleted` — removed from the organization.
Member status. `enabled` — active member; `pending` — invited but not yet accepted; `deleted` — removed from the organization.

member_id.description: Member ID of the member to update

role_ids.description: Role IDs to grant; appended to the member's current roles (duplicates are deduplicated).
Role IDs to remove from the member.

permission_type.description: Whether this is a read or manage permission.

is_granted.description: Present when with_all is true. Indicates whether this permission is granted to the requested roles.

PersonInfosRequest.description: Get person info by IDs request

status.description: Person status. `enabled` — active; `pending` — invited but not yet accepted; `deleted` — removed.

PlatformEmptyObject.description: Empty object returned on success for operations with no meaningful payload.

RoleGrantRequest.description: Request to grant or revoke a role from members.

member_ids.description: Member IDs to grant/revoke the role. Max 100.

role_id.description: Role ID to grant or revoke.

RoleItem.description: A role and its permission set.

permission_ids.description: IDs of permissions granted by this role.

editable.description: False for built-in roles which cannot be modified.

created_at.description: Unix epoch seconds the role was created.

updated_at.description: Unix epoch seconds the role was last updated.

role_ids.description: Filter to permissions granted to these roles.

with_all.description: If true, return all permissions with is_granted set to indicate which are granted.

RoleUpsertRequest.description: Parameters for creating or updating a custom role.

role_id.description: Role ID. Omit or set to 0 to create.

permission_ids.description: Permission IDs to grant. Replaces the existing set.

role_name.description: Role name echoed from the request.

SuccessEnvelope.description: Success response envelope. On every 2xx response, `request_id` identifies the call (also mirrored in the `Flashcat-Request-Id` header) and `data` holds the endpoint-specific payload. Failure responses use a different shape — see `ErrorResponse`.

request_id.description: Unique ID for this request. Mirrored in the Flashcat-Request-Id response header. Include it when reporting issues.

data.description: Endpoint-specific payload. See each operation's 200 response schema.

TeamBriefItem.description: Brief team info for batch responses.

TeamDeleteRequest.description: Request identifying a team to delete.

TeamInfoRequest.description: Request identifying a team by one of ID, name, or external ref.

TeamInfosRequest.description: Request for batch team lookup by IDs.

team_ids.description: List of team IDs to look up. Max 100.

team_name.description: Team display name. 1–39 characters, unique per account.

updated_by_name.description: Display name of the last editor.

updated_by.description: Member ID of the last editor.

created_at.description: Unix epoch seconds the team was created.

updated_at.description: Unix epoch seconds the team was last updated.

ref_id.description: External reference ID for third-party HR system integration.

person_id.description: Filter by member ID — return only teams this person belongs to.

total.description: Total number of teams matching the filter.

TeamUpsertRequest.description: Parameters for creating or updating a team.

team_id.description: Team ID. Omit or set to 0 to create a new team.

person_ids.description: Member IDs to set as team members. Replaces the existing member list.

emails.description: Email addresses to invite as members.

phones.description: Phone numbers to invite as members.

countryCode.description: Default country code applied to any `phones` entries that are not in E.164 format.

ref_id.description: External reference ID for HR system integration.

reset_if_name_exist.description: If true and a team with the same name already exists, reset its membership to the provided person_ids.

team_name.description: Team name echoed from the request.
